chief_complaint: A 72-year-old man presented to the Emergency Department with chest pain.
history: The patient had a history of hypertension and hyperlipidemia. He was admitted to the Emergency Department with a 2-hour history of chest pain. He denied any prior history of coronary artery disease or cardiac surgery. He was on aspirin and atorvastatin.
exam: Vital signs: blood pressure 140/90 mmHg, pulse 90 beats per minute, respiratory rate 18 breaths per minute, oxygen saturation 98% on room air. Physical examination revealed a normal cardiac examination.
labs: ECG: normal sinus rhythm with ST-segment depression in leads II, III, and aVF. Blood work: troponin I 0.5 ng/mL (normal < 0.1 ng/mL). Computed Tomography Angiography (CTA): giant right coronary artery aneurysm (diameter > 8 mm) with mural thrombus and adjacent myocardial infarction.
